subject: Chemistry
title: Jabir ibn Hayyan: Father of Chemistry
description: A micro-lesson that makes Jabir ibn Hayyan: Father of Chemistry usable.

hook.text: Jabir ibn Hayyan invented distillation, crystallization, and named dozens of chemicals still used today.

buildup.text: Working in 8th-century Iraq, Jabir wrote over 300 texts on chemistry, medicine, and philosophy.

discovery.text: He discovered hydrochloric acid, nitric acid, and aqua regia—the only liquid that can dissolve gold.

twist.text: He developed the alembic still—a device for distillation that chemists used for a thousand years after him.

climax.text: The word 'gibberish' may come from 'Jabir'—his writings were so coded that Europeans couldn't understand them.

punchline.text: Acids, distillation, crystallization. One man built chemistry's foundation.

quiz.question: What liquid did Jabir ibn Hayyan discover that can dissolve gold?
options: Aqua regia—a mix of hydrochloric and nitric acid
